Package com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components

Class Account.Builder

java.lang.Object
com.apexfintechsolutions.ascendsdk.models.components.Account.Builder
Enclosing class:
Account
public static final class Account.Builder extends Object

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Account.Builder
    acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication(boolean acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication)
    Indicates if the issuer of a security held by the account is permitted to communicate directly with the shareholder versus through the brokerage firm; This can include sending proxy statements, annual reports, and other important information directly to the shareholder's address on file with the brokerage firm.
    Account.Builder
    acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication(Optional<Boolean> acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication)
    Indicates if the issuer of a security held by the account is permitted to communicate directly with the shareholder versus through the brokerage firm; This can include sending proxy statements, annual reports, and other important information directly to the shareholder's address on file with the brokerage firm.
    Account.Builder
    accountGroupId(String accountGroupId)
    An Account Group is a way of segmenting accounts within a Correspondent; It is up to the client to define what these groups are and AFS Operations is responsible for configuring them; If the client requests additional groups/codes, they can be added; Examples of Account Groups could hypothetically include HNW (High Net Worth), GOLD (Gold Status Customer), and NWC (Northwest Branch Customer)
    Account.Builder
    accountGroupId(Optional<String> accountGroupId)
    An Account Group is a way of segmenting accounts within a Correspondent; It is up to the client to define what these groups are and AFS Operations is responsible for configuring them; If the client requests additional groups/codes, they can be added; Examples of Account Groups could hypothetically include HNW (High Net Worth), GOLD (Gold Status Customer), and NWC (Northwest Branch Customer)
    Account.Builder
    accountId(String accountId)
    A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; This is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions
    Account.Builder
    accountId(Optional<String> accountId)
    A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; This is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions
    Account.Builder
    accountNumber(String accountNumber)
    A "unique" identifier referencing this single account This should be the human readable identifier for the account used for display
    Account.Builder
    accountNumber(Optional<String> accountNumber)
    A "unique" identifier referencing this single account This should be the human readable identifier for the account used for display
    Account.Builder
    activeRestrictions(List<String> activeRestrictions)
    The list of restrictions currently impacting a given account; Restrictions suspend one or more entitlements dependent on their mapping
    Account.Builder
    activeRestrictions(Optional<? extends List<String>> activeRestrictions)
    The list of restrictions currently impacting a given account; Restrictions suspend one or more entitlements dependent on their mapping

    A boolean to indicate if an account is advised.
    Account.Builder
    advised(Optional<Boolean> advised)
    A boolean to indicate if an account is advised.

    The FINRA CAT classification for the Account Holder; Is set automatically based on attributes of the owners and account type

    A unique identifier referencing a Correspondent; A Client may have several operating Correspondents within its purview.

    A unique identifier referencing a Correspondent; A Client may have several operating Correspondents within its purview.

    createTime(OffsetDateTime createTime)
    The time the initial account record was created; May differ from the `open_time` timestamp which depends on when all required data was provided to the system

    The time the initial account record was created; May differ from the `open_time` timestamp which depends on when all required data was provided to the system

    Account.Builder
    fdid(String fdid)
    Apex requires a Firm-Designated ID (FDID) for Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) reporting and uses it as a unique identifier for each trading account; The identifier cannot be the same as the accountID; Apex automatically assigns this ID.
    Account.Builder
    fdid(Optional<String> fdid)
    Apex requires a Firm-Designated ID (FDID) for Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) reporting and uses it as a unique identifier for each trading account; The identifier cannot be the same as the accountID; Apex automatically assigns this ID.
    Account.Builder

    The FINRA COA code associated with the account

    A roll-up account classification based on the `registration_type`; Indicates what owns the account and/or if it is a special type (e.g., Joint, Estate, Retirement, etc.); Used primarily for reporting and high-level type identification

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

  • Method Details

    • acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication

      public Account.Builder acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication(boolean acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication)
      Indicates if the issuer of a security held by the account is permitted to communicate directly with the shareholder versus through the brokerage firm; This can include sending proxy statements, annual reports, and other important information directly to the shareholder's address on file with the brokerage firm. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication

      public Account.Builder acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication(Optional<Boolean> acceptsIssuerDirectCommunication)
      Indicates if the issuer of a security held by the account is permitted to communicate directly with the shareholder versus through the brokerage firm; This can include sending proxy statements, annual reports, and other important information directly to the shareholder's address on file with the brokerage firm. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • accountGroupId

      public Account.Builder accountGroupId(String accountGroupId)
      An Account Group is a way of segmenting accounts within a Correspondent; It is up to the client to define what these groups are and AFS Operations is responsible for configuring them; If the client requests additional groups/codes, they can be added; Examples of Account Groups could hypothetically include HNW (High Net Worth), GOLD (Gold Status Customer), and NWC (Northwest Branch Customer)

    • accountGroupId

      public Account.Builder accountGroupId(Optional<String> accountGroupId)
      An Account Group is a way of segmenting accounts within a Correspondent; It is up to the client to define what these groups are and AFS Operations is responsible for configuring them; If the client requests additional groups/codes, they can be added; Examples of Account Groups could hypothetically include HNW (High Net Worth), GOLD (Gold Status Customer), and NWC (Northwest Branch Customer)

    • accountId

      public Account.Builder accountId(String accountId)
      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; This is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions

      public Account.Builder accountId(Optional<String> accountId)
      A globally unique identifier referencing a single account; This is the main identifier for an account used for machine-to-machine interactions

      A "unique" identifier referencing this single account This should be the human readable identifier for the account used for display

      A "unique" identifier referencing this single account This should be the human readable identifier for the account used for display

    • cftcOwnerType

      public Account.Builder cftcOwnerType(CftcOwnerType cftcOwnerType)
      Indicates the CFTC (Commodity Futures Trading Commission) owner type of the account. This enum only applies to accounts regulated by the CFTC

    • cftcOwnerType

      public Account.Builder cftcOwnerType(Optional<? extends CftcOwnerType> cftcOwnerType)
      Indicates the CFTC (Commodity Futures Trading Commission) owner type of the account. This enum only applies to accounts regulated by the CFTC

    • clientAccountId

      public Account.Builder clientAccountId(String clientAccountId)
      An external identifier for the account. This identifier does not have internal uniqueness constraints.

      An external identifier for the account. This identifier does not have internal uniqueness constraints.

    • fdid

      public Account.Builder fdid(String fdid)
      Apex requires a Firm-Designated ID (FDID) for Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) reporting and uses it as a unique identifier for each trading account; The identifier cannot be the same as the accountID; Apex automatically assigns this ID.

    • fdid

      public Account.Builder fdid(Optional<String> fdid)
      Apex requires a Firm-Designated ID (FDID) for Consolidated Audit Trail (CAT) reporting and uses it as a unique identifier for each trading account; The identifier cannot be the same as the accountID; Apex automatically assigns this ID.

      public Account.Builder marginGroupId(String marginGroupId)
      An identifier generated for all non-cash accounts; Uses a combination of account type and owner information to tie accounts together in order to perform margin calculations

      The previous account ID associated with the account; Must be unique

    • ownershipType

      public Account.Builder ownershipType(OwnershipType ownershipType)
      A roll-up account classification based on the `registration_type`; Indicates what owns the account and/or if it is a special type (e.g., Joint, Estate, Retirement, etc.); Used primarily for reporting and high-level type identification

    • ownershipType

      public Account.Builder ownershipType(Optional<? extends OwnershipType> ownershipType)
      A roll-up account classification based on the `registration_type`; Indicates what owns the account and/or if it is a special type (e.g., Joint, Estate, Retirement, etc.); Used primarily for reporting and high-level type identification

    • parties

      public Account.Builder parties(List<Party> parties)
      Parties associated with the account (e.g. custodian).

    • parties

      public Account.Builder parties(Optional<? extends List<Party>> parties)
      Parties associated with the account (e.g. custodian).

    • patternDayTrader

      public Account.Builder patternDayTrader(boolean patternDayTrader)
      Indicates if the customer is a PDT; This is set if the account executes four or more day trades (buy and sell the same security intraday) within a period of five business days. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • patternDayTrader

      public Account.Builder patternDayTrader(Optional<Boolean> patternDayTrader)
      Indicates if the customer is a PDT; This is set if the account executes four or more day trades (buy and sell the same security intraday) within a period of five business days. By default, this is set to `false`.

      The primary registered representative for the account

      The primary registered representative for the account

    • registrationType

      public Account.Builder registrationType(RegistrationType registrationType)
      The registration of the account (e.g. Individual, Joint, IRA, etc.)

    • registrationType

      public Account.Builder registrationType(Optional<? extends RegistrationType> registrationType)
      The registration of the account (e.g. Individual, Joint, IRA, etc.)

      The Reserve Class associated with the account

    • taxProfile

      public Account.Builder taxProfile(AccountTaxProfile taxProfile)
      Tax-related attributes for the account; A tax profile with taxpayer attributes is located on the `legal_natural_person` or `legal_entity` objects elsewhere on the account record

      Tax-related attributes for the account; A tax profile with taxpayer attributes is located on the `legal_natural_person` or `legal_entity` objects elsewhere on the account record

      A list of persons designated to verify the well being of the account holder.

    • wrapFeeBilled

      public Account.Builder wrapFeeBilled(boolean wrapFeeBilled)
      A boolean to indicate if an account is a wrap brokerage account. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • wrapFeeBilled

      public Account.Builder wrapFeeBilled(Optional<Boolean> wrapFeeBilled)
      A boolean to indicate if an account is a wrap brokerage account. By default, this is set to `false`.

    • build

      public Account build()
